title: Classify Simplified Implementations
summary: Classifies source-backed simplified implementation surfaces that execute local TypeScript but remain scoped to the current launcher path, product adapter contracts, or prior-plan manifest authority rather than final vanilla DOOM 1.9 end-to-end parity.
evidence_method: inventory lane source-only audit using git ls-files counts over src/main.ts, src/launcher/, src/playable/, test/launcher/, and non-acceptance test/playable/ roots, with supporting evidence from package.json, current tests, and prior plan artifacts listed in the selected step read-only scope.

criteria_sorted: backs an executable TypeScript module or Bun test instead of only a plan manifest
depends on current launcher contracts, prior-plan audit manifests, or product adapters rather than the final vanilla D_DoomMain path
does not claim paired reference/implementation parity or full end-to-end vanilla behavior
records local source evidence that must be replaced or proven by later parity steps
classification_definition.scope_note: simplified means source-backed and locally testable, but not sufficient by itself as a final vanilla DOOM 1.9 behavior claim.

simplified_implementation_groups.evidence: src/main.ts exposes the DOOM Codex launcher command path, defaults to E1M1, parses launcher-specific flags, and invokes src/launcher/win32.ts instead of importing src/mainLoop.ts or a D_DoomMain/D_DoomLoop orchestrator.
simplified_implementation_groups.notes: This is the current playable local surface and has executable launcher tests, but it is intentionally not the final vanilla startup path.
simplification_markers: package.json start script is bun run src/main.ts rather than bun run doom.ts
src/launcher/session.ts enables automap cheating level 2 for the launcher view
src/main.ts help identifies the surface as DOOM Codex launcher
src/main.ts launches gameplay directly and offers Tab automap toggle instead of vanilla title/menu startup

simplified_implementation_groups.evidence: src/playable modules provide executable adapter and contract surfaces for the planned playable product path, but many entries encode runtime-command guards, prior plan audit manifest links, and current launcher handoffs instead of direct final vanilla parity execution.
simplified_implementation_groups.notes: The modules are real TypeScript and have matching Bun tests, but the group is classified separately because it adapts the current launcher and prior-plan contracts while final parity gates remain pending.
simplification_markers: representative modules guard on bun run doom.ts while delegating to src/main.ts or src/launcher surfaces
representative modules retain sourceAuditPath or sourceAuditManifest references to plan_fps artifacts
representative tests prove adapter contracts and deterministic traces, not paired reference process parity

excluded_surfaces.reason: Acceptance gate tests under this root are gate/manifest checks without a matching src/playable acceptance implementation root.
Oracle fixtures are evidence artifacts or pending capture contracts, not simplified implementation code.
Runtime implementation roots outside src/main.ts, src/launcher/, and src/playable/ were already classified as source-backed real implementation groups.

implications: 01-023 must still classify manifest-only, pending, or stubbed surfaces such as acceptance gates and oracle fixtures.
01-024 should treat this artifact as source-only inventory metadata, not as a final parity gate.
Future launch and acceptance steps must replace the current launcher shortcut with bun run doom.ts and the vanilla D_DoomMain/D_DoomLoop path before claiming end-to-end parity.
